skill.name: Azure API Management SDK for Python
skill.description: This skill provides a comprehensive guide to using the Azure API Management SDK for Python. It helps developers create, manage, and publish APIs through Azure API Management service using Python code.
skill.summary: A guide for using Azure API Management SDK to manage APIs in Python
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and guidance for the Azure API Management SDK for Python. The static analyzer scanned 0 files and detected 0 potential security issues. The skill provides educational content about Azure API Management without any executable code that could pose security risks.

content.user_title: Manage Azure APIs with Python SDK
content.value_statement: This skill helps developers integrate and manage Azure API Management services using Python, enabling automated API deployment and management workflows.
actual_capabilities: Generate Python code for Azure API Management operations
Create and configure API Management instances
limitations: Does not provide live Azure API Management credentials
Cannot execute actual API calls without user configuration
Requires valid Azure subscription for real-world usage
Generated code templates require Azure SDK installation

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Automating API Deployments
use_cases.description: Automate the creation and configuration of API Management instances as part of CI/CD pipelines using Python scripts.

use_cases.title: Backend Developer Building API Gateways
use_cases.description: Use the SDK to programmatically create APIs, configure rate limiting, and set up authentication for backend services.

use_cases.title: Platform Engineer Managing API Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Manage multiple API Management instances, products, and subscriptions across different environments.
use_cases.target_user: Platform engineers responsible for API infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic API in Azure API Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a new API in Azure API Management using the Python SDK. Include code for setting up the API, adding operations, and configuring a backend service.

prompt_templates.title: Configure API authentication policies
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I configure JWT validation and OAuth2 authentication policies for an API in Azure API Management using Python?

prompt_templates.title: Import OpenAPI specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Python code to import an OpenAPI 3.0 specification into Azure API Management and generate documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Set up rate limiting and quotas
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to configure rate limiting policies and product subscription quotas using the Azure API Management Python SDK.

output_examples.input: Create an API Management service instance
output_examples.output:
```python
from azure.mgmt.apimanagement import ApiManagementClient
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

# Initialize the client
credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
subscription_id = "your-subscription-id"
resource_group_name = "your-resource-group"
service_name = "your-api-service"

client = ApiManagementClient(credential, subscription_id)

# Create the API Management service
poller = client.api_management_service.begin_create_or_update(
    resource_group_name,
    service_name,
    {
        'location': 'eastus',
        'publisher_name': 'Your Company',
        'publisher_email': 'admin@company.com',
        'sku_name': 'Developer',
        'sku_capacity': 1
    }
)
result = poller.result()
print(f"API Management service created: {result.name}")
```

```python
# Register a new API in API Management
api_id = "my-new-api"
client.api.begin_create_or_update(
    resource_group_name,
    service_name,
    api_id,
    {
        'display_name': 'My New API',
        'path': 'myapi',
        'service_url': 'https://api.example.com',
        'protocols': ['https']
    }
)
```

```python
# Apply rate limiting policy
client.api_policy.create_or_update(
    resource_group_name,
    service_name,
    api_id,
    {
        'format': 'xml',
        'value': '<policies><inbound><rate-limit-by-key calls="100" renewal-period="60" counter-key="@(context.Request.IpAddress)" /></inbound></policies>'
    }
)
```

best_practices: Use managed identities for authentication instead of storing credentials in code
Organize APIs by products and use subscription keys for access control
Apply versioning to APIs from the start to enable smooth updates
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode subscription keys or secrets in source code
Avoid configuring policies directly in production without testing
Do not expose backend services directly without API Management layer

faq.question: What is Azure API Management?
faq.answer: Azure API Management is a cloud service that helps organizations publish, manage, secure, and analyze APIs. It provides a gateway for API traffic, caching, authentication, and logging.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: You do not need an Azure subscription to learn from this skill. However, to execute the generated code, you will need an active Azure subscription.

faq.question: What Python version is supported?
faq.answer: The Azure API Management SDK for Python supports Python 3.7 and later versions.

faq.question: Can this skill help with API versioning?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes guidance on setting up and managing API versions using Azure API Management.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure?
faq.answer: You can use Azure Active Directory, managed identities, or service principal credentials. The recommended approach is using DefaultAzureCredential for local development.

faq.question: Can I import existing OpenAPI specifications?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure API Management supports importing OpenAPI 2.0 and 3.0 specifications, and this skill covers how to do it programmatically.
